result:
All 6 tests pass. There were two bugs in `lrucache/cache.py`:

1. **`get` didn't refresh recency** (cache.py:30): a hit returned the value without moving the entry to the MRU end of the `OrderedDict`, so a just-read key could still be evicted as the "least recently used". Fixed by adding `move_to_end(key)` on a non-expired hit.

2. **`put` evicted on updates** (cache.py:33): the eviction check ran whenever the cache was at capacity, even when overwriting an existing key — which doesn't grow the cache. Updating `"y"` in a full 2-entry cache wrongly evicted `"x"`. Fixed by only evicting when the key is new: `if key not in self._data and len(self._data) >= self.capacity`.

Both fixes are explained in `SOLUTION.md`, and `python3 -m pytest -q` reports **6 passed**.
